title: UX plan (user-facing flows and Given-When-Then acceptance scenarios)
description: What the user experiences. Sibling of spec (which is system-facing). Holds primary user flows, acceptance scenarios in Given-When-Then form, states, and edge cases.

steps.description: Methodology may require an activation modality tag prefix (e.g. `[keyboard|pointer-mouse] ...`). See plan-ux/PRINCIPLES.md §1.

flow_ref.description: Optional reference to a primary_flows[].id this scenario validates.

counterexample.description: Methodology §8 (plan-ux): description of a wrong implementation that would still pass this scenario. Empty if none can be constructed (= scenario is discriminating).

vs_legacy.description: Methodology §9 (plan-ux): only meaningful in refactor/port/migration context. must-fail = the legacy implementation would fail this scenario (delta encoding); must-pass = legacy behavior intentionally preserved; irrelevant = new territory.

reference_ux.description: Methodology §8 (plan-ux): UX patterns this design models on or rejects. Names may be products (Raycast / Spotlight / Linear) or UI primitives (combobox / breadcrumb).

legacy_context.description: Methodology §9 (plan-ux): present only when this UX is a refactor / port / migration of an existing implementation. Encodes what is inherited vs replaced.
